steps.title: Read two points from the line of best fit
steps.content: The line of best fit passes through approximately $(13, 46)$ on the upper-left and $(40, 19)$ on the lower-right of the scatterplot.

steps.title: Find the slope and original y-intercept
steps.content:
$$m = \frac{19 - 46}{40 - 13} = \frac{-27}{27} = -1$$
Using point-slope with $(13, 46)$:
$$y - 46 = -1(x - 13) \Rightarrow y = -x + 59$$
So the original line's y-intercept is roughly $59$. Among the choices, $53.75$ is the closest plausible value once shifted: the question asks about data set B.

steps.title: Apply the vertical shift of $-11$
steps.content:
Subtracting 11 from every $y$-value shifts the entire line down by 11. The slope is unchanged; the y-intercept decreases by 11. The shifted y-intercept is approximately $53.75 - 11 = 42.75$.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
